text: <p><em>This paper describes personal and communal attitudes of Maduranese towards carok and the reasons of existing carok. The study’s approach is </em><em>descri</em><em>ptive involving 1</em><em>95 Maduranese. Observation, questionnaire, and interview were implemented to collect data. Result of analysis shows 4 personal attitudes toward caro</em><em>k as follow: 75% accept</em><em> that </em><em>they are not intend to do carok, 60% accept</em><em> that they will not do carok in any reason</em><em>, 77.38% accept </em><em>to solv</em><em>e all cases wisely without doing carok</em><em>, and (4) 77.40% </em><em>will not do carok because of being obedient to state and religious rules</em><em>. While 5 Maduranese communal attitudes toward carok involve</em><em>: 64.16% accept</em><em> that carok is not Madura tradition, 81.11% accept</em><em> that Madura community lov</em><em>e peace, 86.11%</em><em> agree that carok does not represent Maduranese</em><em>, 82.44% </em><em>agree that carok is</em><em> bad</em><em> rules, and 76.11% accept</em><em> to solv</em><em>e all cases wisely without carok</em><em>. It is also concluded that the motivation of carok</em><em> are women, misunderstanding, inheritance, belief, theft, and debt</em><em> problems.</em></p>
